pattern: Two numbers are digit-rearrangements of each other exactly when their digit multisets match, so compare the number's sorted digits against every power of two with the same digit count.
hints: Reordering digits never changes how many of each digit you have, so the multiset of digits is the only thing that survives a rearrangement.
There are only about thirty powers of two below the limit; a power of two is reachable iff one of them has the very same collection of digits.
Compute a digit fingerprint (such as the sorted digit string or a count of each digit) for the input, then test it against the fingerprint of each power of two until one matches.

edge_inputs.description: first example: one is itself a power of two
second example: ten cannot be reordered into a power of two
sixteen is already a power of two
24 shares no power of two digit multiset
largest power of two within the range
a permutation of 8192 is itself a power of two

stress.description: random integers spanning the whole range, mostly false
